description: Intention-lane normalization tables for the mechanical harvest (ct-intention-extraction.md §7.1). The text-lane sibling of src/ctkr/normalization.json: that file folds STRUCTURAL idioms before the name-blind math; this one folds NAMING/COMMENT idioms after the structure is frozen, so the intention channel compares token sequences and semantics rather than raw source strings. All entries are DATA (versioned) — adding a language or an affix is a table edit, not code. Raw text is always retained alongside the normalized form (§7.1 'raw text always retained').

harvest_exclusions.note: Path prefixes/globs dropped from the intention harvest by default (in ADDITION to the CLI --exclude-prefix). These are harness, smoke-test, and one-off spike files: not product code, and their thin single-token naming pollutes role-class A5 patterns, producing spurious structure-vs-intention 'tension' verdicts in T5b adjudication (§5.2/§6.1). A versioned DATA dial — widen it here, never in code. Matched on the symbol's repo-relative file: `path_prefixes` by startswith, `path_globs` by the same minimal glob grammar as test_conventions. Real test files (*.test.ts etc.) are deliberately NOT excluded — they carry the S1 behavioral signal.

convention_affixes.note: §7.1(2) — per-language/framework affixes that carry CONVENTION rather than DOMAIN. Folded out of identifier token sequences before A5 naming-pattern extraction and before cross-stack comparison. `where` = prefix|suffix|token (matched on the tokenized, lowercased sequence). `portability` tags what survives a port: 'N' = convention-encoded (restate, e.g. an interface's I-prefix), 'A' = idiom-specific (drop, e.g. a Python dunder). Domain tokens (no entry) default to intent-I.

ts.reason: I-prefixed interface convention (only when followed by PascalCase, handled by the tokenizer's single-letter guard)

marker_vocabulary.note: §7.1(3) — marker comments normalized into one canonical scenario form. Each canonical marker carries a `meta` reading used by §6 (HACK = structure & intention knowingly diverge; TODO = intent not yet realized). Matched case-insensitively as a whole word.

meta.TODO: intent not yet realized; the port must decide, not stumble
meta.FIXME: known-broken intent; the port inherits a decision
meta.SAFETY: non-obvious constraint the shape does not show
meta.PERF: performance rationale for an otherwise-puzzling shape

test_conventions.note: §1 S1 / §7.1(3) — test-file detection and test-name grammars, per ecosystem. `path_globs` are matched on the repo-relative file path; `import_markers` corroborate (a file importing a framework). `name_prefixes` mark test *symbols* within a test file. Deterministic, no framework execution.

name_semantics.note: §6.1 — read/write-implying name morphemes used by the mechanical conflict detectors and by the S2 identifier harvest's semantic hint. Matched against the FOLDED token sequence (tokenizer output), so `getUserById` and `get_user_by_id` hit the same rule. These are HINTS about claimed behavior; structure owns what actually happens (§6.2).

string_classifiers.note: §1 A2 — cheap regex classifiers over string literals harvested from member source slices (regex approximation of tree-sitter string nodes; see coverage note in the harvest output). Ordered; first match wins. `portability` is the default intent tier for that class.

scoring.note: §5.2 D/R composite weights and §5.3 class thresholds. ALL DIALS (entropy-as-dial), overridable at the CLI. Shipped defaults, calibrated on the first real port (§5.3), NOT truths.

kind_discriminativeness.note: per-edge-kind weight for the D 'profile information mass' term. CONTAINS is scaffolding (says almost nothing); crossing/behavioral kinds are discriminative. Mirrors the src/ctkr down-weighting instinct.

portability_defaults.note: §7.2 — default intent-tier per indicator kind; the affix/marker tables catch the N/A residue. I=universal (into the brief as-is), N=convention-encoded (restate), A=idiom-specific (drop, retained in provenance).
